title: Ellipsis not working properly when a text overflows via constrained height instead of max lines
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Flutter project
2. Paste the code sample into `main.dart`
3. Run on any platform

### Expected results

Ellipsis should show up at the end of the text if the text is cut off by its parent's constrained height, even if `maxLines` is null or a large value.

### Actual results

Ellipsis shows up when `maxLines` is null, but then it trims the text into one line. Ellipsis does not show at all if `maxLines` is greater than what could normally fit in the constrained size.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const App());

class App extends StatelessWidget {
  const App({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: ExamplePage(),
    );
  }
}

class ExamplePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const ExamplePage({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    final text =
        'Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore eu fugiat nulla pariatur. Excepteur sint occaecat cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollit anim id est laborum.';

    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Example')),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          spacing: 16,
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: [
            Text('Max lines: null'),
            Container(
              width: 200,
              height: 200,
              color: Colors.blue,
              child: Text(
                text,
                overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
                maxLines: null,
              ),
            ),
            Text('Max lines: 100'),
            Container(
              width: 200,
              height: 200,
              color: Colors.blue,
              child: Text(
                text,
                overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
                maxLines: 100,
              ),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>


</details>

comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on stable 3.29 and master 3.32 on all platforms — ellipsis is not shown when text is clipped by a constrained parent height rather than by `maxLines`. One commenter noted it may be working as intended but acknowledged it is hard to work around. A workaround using `LayoutBuilder` and `TextPainter` to compute the number of visible lines dynamically was provided; no framework fix or PR is linked.

title: The framework should provide a mechanism to change the paragraph spacing of text
body:
## Issue
There is no mechanism to change the paragraph spacing of text in the framework.

## Proposal
`TextStyle` should add a `paragraphSpacing` member, similar to `letterSpacing` and `wordSpacing`, that allows for changing the additional space between paragraphs.

In the code below:
```dart
Text('Hello world\nGoodbye world', style: TextStyle(paragraphSpacing: 2.0))
```

`TextStyle(paragraphSpacing: 2.0)` , would add 2 pixels of space between the paragraphs separated by the line terminator `\n`.

## Use case
Accessibility would benefit greatly from this feature, as certain specifications such as [WCAG](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#visual-presentation) on the web have certain requirements regarding the paragraph spacing of text.

title: The framework should apply `paragraphSpacingOverride` to its text
body:
## Issue
As of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/172915, `paragraphSpacingOverride` has been made available through `MediaQuery.maybeOfParagraphSpacingOverride`, and `MediaQueryData.paragraphSpacingOverride`. This is an API added to support accessibility use-cases related to text spacing, such as matching WCAG specifications on the web. The framework at this time does not utilize this value, but makes it accessible to developers to use in their own applications.

## Proposal
The framework should apply the `paragraphSpacingOverride` to its own text and components, so the framework can be compliant with specifications like WCAG out of the box. When applying `paragraphSpacingOverride` to our framework components we should consider scenarios where we should not apply it, for example in buttons, headers, navigations destinations, and likely other UI components.

title: iOS: NSJSONSerialization crash when deleting SMP characters
body:
**Describe the bug**
When deleting Supplementary Multilingual Plane (SMP) characters on iOS, the text input system leaves orphaned UTF-16 surrogates in the NSString. This causes NSJSONSerialization to fail with "failed to convert to UTF8" error and crash the app.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a Flutter app with a TextField or text input widget
2. On an iOS device, paste this string: **ᴿ᭄𝑹ahul Rajput**
3. Delete it character by character until only 1-2 characters remain
4. App crashes with: "Invalid JSON message, encoding failed: (null)"

**Expected behavior**
Text input should handle surrogate pairs correctly without crashing

**Actual behavior**
NSJSONSerialization rejects the string because it contains lone (unpaired) surrogates from incomplete SMP character encoding

**Proposed Solution**
Before JSON serialization, validate and sanitize UTF-16 surrogates:
- Drop lone/orphaned surrogates
- Keep valid surrogate pairs
- Preserve all BMP characters
- Remap text-editing cursor indices

**Code Fix**
The fix has been implemented in the Engine layer (FlutterCodecs.mm):
- Added `sanitizeJSONObject()` function to recursively clean JSON object trees
- Added helper functions for surrogate detection and index remapping
- Special handling for text-editing state dicts

Commit: `5ffdf6f689e18fe0f6b3045f0b07453d4a0c3266`
Branch: `fix/ios-surrogate-utf16-crash`

**Platform**
- iOS 12.0+

**Related Issues**
GitHub Issue #142327
comment_summary: Reproduced on both latest stable and master on iOS. A contributor implemented a fix in the engine (`FlutterCodecs.mm`) on a fork and submitted to Gerrit mirrors; a team member redirected them to submit a PR against the monorepo at `engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/common/framework/Source/FlutterCodecs.mm`. A pre-triage note suggests it may be a bug in `UITextInput.deleteBackward` rather than a duplicate of #179727. Status: P1, has a proposed fix pending a properly submitted PR.
